cards.title: Immune System — Overview for Naturopathic Practice
cards._legacyHtml:
Immune Architecture
The immune system operates across two arms: innate immunity (immediate, non-specific — first 96 hours) and adaptive immunity (specific, memory-forming — days to weeks). Naturopathic immune modulation works primarily through: gut microbiome optimisation (70% of immune cells are gut-associated), nutritional support (Vit D, zinc, Vit C are rate-limiting immune cofactors), sleep (immune consolidation during deep sleep), stress reduction (cortisol is profoundly immunosuppressive), and targeted botanicals.
ComponentRoleKey Naturopathic Support
NK cells (Natural Killer)First-line tumour and viral surveillance. Kill without prior sensitisationMedicinal mushrooms (beta-glucans ★★★), forest bathing ★★, zinc, Vit D, sleep
Th1 cellsIntracellular pathogens (viral, bacterial). IFN-γ, TNF-α productionAstragalus, echinacea, elderberry, adequate protein intake, Vit D
Th2 cellsParasites, allergy, asthma — IL-4, IL-5, IL-13. Excess Th2 = atopic diseaseReduce: sugar, trans fats, processed food. Increase: omega-3, Vit D (Th2 → Treg shift)
Th17 cellsMucosal defence, gut barrier. Excess Th17 = autoimmunity (RA, IBD, MS, psoriasis)Gut microbiome (Clostridium species → Treg induction). Vit D ★★★ suppresses Th17
Treg cellsImmune tolerance — prevent autoimmunity and allergy. IL-10, TGF-β productionVit D ★★★ (most potent Treg inducer), butyrate (dietary fibre), FMT, probiotics
B cells / AntibodiesHumoral immunity — IgG (long-term), IgA (mucosal), IgM (acute), IgE (allergy)Adequate protein (immunoglobulin synthesis requires amino acids), zinc, selenium
Secretory IgA (SIgA)Primary mucosal defence — respiratory, GI, urogenital tracts. First barrier against pathogensProbiotics (Lactobacillus ★★★ — ↑ SIgA), colostrum, reduce stress (cortisol ↓ SIgA)

cards.title: Immune Enhancement — Evidence-Based Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
1
Nutritional Immune Foundations Vitamin D3 2000–4000 IU/day ★★★ (VDR on every immune cell — deficiency = 14× ↑ respiratory infection risk, immunosuppressed T/B/NK cells). Zinc 15–30mg/day ★★★ (required for thymulin production — thymus hormone, T cell maturation; reduces common cold duration 40% if taken within 24hrs of onset). Vitamin C 1–2g/day ★★★ (neutrophil chemotaxis, NK cell activity, antiviral — IV Vit C in sepsis reduces mortality). Selenium 100–200mcg/day ★★★ (glutathione peroxidase — viral mutation suppression; deficiency allows benign viruses to mutate to virulence). Protein ≥0.8g/kg/day (antibody synthesis, immune cell proliferation — severe deficiency = immune collapse).
2
Botanical Immune Modulators Echinacea purpurea (standardised extract) 900mg/day at onset ★★★ (reduces cold duration by 1.5 days, severity by 23%). Elderberry (Sambucus nigra) 15ml QID for 5 days ★★★ (flavonoids inhibit viral cell entry; meta-analysis: reduces flu duration by 4 days and severity significantly). Astragalus 1000mg/day (Th1 immune stimulant, NK cell activator — best for prevention, not acute treatment). Medicinal mushrooms: Reishi (Ganoderma — immunomodulatory, NK cell ↑), Shiitake (lentinan — ↑ NK cells, antiviral), Turkey Tail (PSK/PSP — used in Japanese cancer immune support). Beta-glucan from oats/yeast 250–500mg/day (innate immune priming — reduces upper respiratory infections in athletes ★★★).
3
Gut-Immune Axis Optimisation 70% of immune cells (GALT — gut-associated lymphoid tissue) reside in the gut. Probiotics: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG ★★★ (most studied — reduces respiratory infections, antibiotic-associated diarrhoea). Lactobacillus acidophilus NCFM + Bifidobacterium lactis ★★ (↑ SIgA, ↑ NK cells in winter cold prevention studies). Prebiotics (inulin, FOS, GOS) → butyrate → Treg induction → immune tolerance. Fermented foods: kefir (most evidence), kimchi, yogurt, miso — increase microbiome diversity → immune resilience.
ScenarioPriority Protocol
Recurrent infections (colds 4+/year)Vit D, zinc, echinacea (prevention), gut microbiome, sleep 8hrs, stress reduction. Rule out: IgA deficiency, diabetes, haematological causes
Post-illness recoveryVit C 3g/day, glutamine 5g/day (gut barrier repair), colostrum, elderberry, rest priority, protein repletion
Immune support during chemotherapyIntegrative oncology consultation — NOT all immune supplements safe during chemo. Vit D and zinc generally safe. Avoid echinacea, astragalus (may interfere with immunosuppressive chemo). Refer SIO guidelines

cards.title: Autoimmunity — Naturopathic Framework
cards._legacyHtml:
⚠️ Autoimmunity — Naturopathy as Adjunct
Autoimmune conditions require conventional medical management (disease-modifying drugs, biologic agents). Naturopathic interventions are evidence-based adjuncts that reduce inflammation, improve quality of life, manage side effects, and may reduce flares. NEVER advise stopping prescribed immunosuppressants. Always work in collaboration with the patient's rheumatologist, neurologist, or specialist.
Autoimmune ConditionNaturopathic PriorityKey InterventionsEvidence
Rheumatoid ArthritisInflammation reduction, gut microbiome, joint nutritionOmega-3 3–4g/day ★★★ (reduces NSAID requirement, ↓ DAS28 score), turmeric 500mg TID ★★★, elimination diet (trial: gluten-free, dairy-free for 12 weeks), Boswellia 400mg TID ★★★★★ Omega-3 meta-analysis: significant joint pain and stiffness reduction
Hashimoto's ThyroiditisAntioxidant support, gut barrier, molecular mimicry reductionSelenium 200mcg/day ★★★ (↓ TPO antibodies 50% — Cochrane), Vit D 4000IU/day, gluten-free trial ★★, myo-inositol 600mg BD, LDN (low dose naltrexone — conventional adjunct)★★★ Selenium: most evidence-based naturopathic intervention in autoimmunity
Inflammatory Bowel DiseaseGut barrier restoration, microbiome, inflammationCurcumin 3g/day ★★★ (UC — RCT equal to mesalamine for maintenance), omega-3 ★★, Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG ★★, glutamine 10g/day, exclusive enteral nutrition (Crohn's), aloe vera 100ml BD (UC)★★★ Multiple high-quality RCTs support curcumin in UC
Multiple SclerosisNeuroprotection, Vit D, anti-inflammatoryVit D 10,000 IU/day (Coimbra protocol — under supervision), omega-3 3g/day, Mediterranean diet, exercise ★★★, mindfulness ★★, alpha-lipoic acid 1200mg/day★★★ Exercise: most evidence-based non-pharmacological MS intervention (Cochrane)
Systemic Lupus (SLE)Photoprotection, anti-inflammatory, renal supportOmega-3 3g/day ★★, Vit D, antimalarial diet (avoid: alfalfa sprouts — stimulates autoimmunity), sun protection ★★★ (UV triggers flares). N-acetylcysteine (NAC) — emerging evidence for lupus nephritis★★ Photoprotection is the strongest naturopathic intervention for SLE flare prevention
PsoriasisGut-skin axis, inflammation, stressOmega-3 3–4g/day ★★★, Vit D topical + systemic, turmeric ★★, elimination diet, stress management ★★★ (psoriasis is profoundly stress-reactive), probiotic (Lactobacillus ★★), milk thistle (liver support)★★★ Omega-3 and stress reduction: strongest naturopathic evidence in psoriasis
🟡 The Gut-Autoimmunity Connection
Leaky gut (intestinal hyperpermeability) is increasingly implicated in autoimmune disease development (Fasano's zonulin hypothesis). Zonulin (regulated by gliadin/gluten and bacterial LPS) opens tight junctions → undigested antigens cross into systemic circulation → molecular mimicry → autoimmune activation. Naturopathic gut barrier restoration: L-glutamine 5–10g/day, zinc carnosine 75mg/day, collagen peptides, slippery elm, DGL liquorice, remove triggers (gluten trial, processed food elimination), probiotics. Testing: urinary lactulose:mannitol ratio (intestinal permeability test).

cards.title: Allergy & Hypersensitivity — Naturopathic Management
cards._legacyHtml:
Allergy TypeMechanismNaturopathic ApproachEvidence
Allergic RhinitisIgE-mediated mast cell degranulation on mucosal surfaceQuercetin 500mg BD (mast cell stabiliser ★★), butterbur 75mg BD ★★★ (comparable to cetirizine, no sedation), local honey (controversial — theoretical desensitisation), probiotics ★★, nasal saline irrigation ★★★★★★ Butterbur: multicentre RCT comparable to antihistamine
Food Allergy (IgE)Immediate IgE-mediated — anaphylaxis riskAllergy testing (RAST/skin prick), strict avoidance, adrenaline autoinjector (EpiPen) training, gut microbiome optimisation (reduces risk in infancy)Prevention: early introduction of allergenic foods in infancy (LEAP trial ★★★ — reduces peanut allergy 81%)
Food Sensitivity (IgG/non-IgE)Delayed reaction (hours–days), IgG-mediated, gut permeabilityIgG food sensitivity panel + elimination-reintroduction protocol (gold standard). Gut barrier restoration. Digestive enzymes. Rotation diet.★★ IgG testing controversial — most useful when combined with clinical elimination-challenge protocol
Histamine Intolerance↓ Diamine oxidase (DAO) enzyme activity → histamine accumulation from dietary sourcesLow-histamine diet (avoid: fermented foods, aged cheese, wine, fish sauce, leftovers). DAO enzyme supplement with meals. Quercetin (DAO cofactor). Vit B6, copper (DAO cofactors). Heal gut (DAO produced by intestinal epithelium)★★ DAO supplementation: symptomatic improvement in RCTs. Low-histamine diet effective in 2–4 weeks
